form.createDescription: Fill in the basic information to start your creative journey

metadata.title: METADATA
metadata.description: This is the metadata of your novel, used for organizing your novel collection.
metadata.author: Author / Pen name

cover.title: COVER
cover.description: This is the cover of your novel. It will be displayed on the novel collection page.
cover.dragDrop: or drag and drop on the area

writing.labelsDescription: Use these to organize your scenes by status, subplot, etc. You can also prefix them with a group (e.g. "Status: Draft").
writing.languageDescription: This is the language of your novel. It will be used for spell checking and hyphenation.

memory.outlineDescription: Controls how '{{ novel.outline }}' assembles your outline in prompts.
memory.collapseChaptersDescription: When a volume (act) has a summary, use it to stand in for that volume and skip the chapter/scene summaries underneath; volumes without a summary still list their chapters. When off, the volume summary is followed by every chapter summary in that volume. The current volume is never collapsed, so its progress is always kept.

codex.connectionsDescription: Store isolated Codex homes per account so each connection persists its own auth.json and config.toml.
codex.draftDescription: Fill the config first, then save it as an isolated Codex connection.

providerTypeHints.openaiOfficial: Authorize Codex with a ChatGPT account and write auth.json automatically.
providerTypeHints.custom: Manage auth.json and config.toml manually for experiments or special setups.

codex.officialUrlHint: Official connections finish ChatGPT authorization in the browser. No API key is needed here.
codex.officialAuthDeploymentHint: Use Device Code authorization for server deployments; use ChatGPT authorization for local development.
codex.deviceCodeTitle: Enter this code on the authorization page
codex.noCustomModels: No models yet. Fetch the list first.
codex.authJsonHint: Codex account authorization is persisted in this isolated file.
codex.configTomlHint: Codex runtime config is saved together with this connection.
codex.confirmDelete: Delete this Codex connection? This also removes its isolated config directory.

errors.saveFailed: Failed to save the Codex connection.

errors.missingCustomApiKey: Please enter an API key first.

ai.connectionsDescription: Add providers and fetch models. Credentials are encrypted and stored on the server.
ai.apiKeyHint: Your API Key will be encrypted and stored on the server
ai.baseUrlHint: Modify this if using OpenAI-compatible services (Azure, local models, etc.)
ai.modelSetsDescription: Drag model groups into sets to organize them for later use (writing, summary, etc.).
ai.availableGroupsHint: Drag a model group into a set to include it.
ai.modelGroupsDescription: Drag models into groups and configure call behavior.
ai.providersHint: Drag a model into a group to assign it.
ai.modelTypesAutoDetectHint: The detected result is used by default until you change it manually. Manual type changes can affect capability checks, so leave the default if you are unsure.

sidebar.chatEmptyDescription: Start a new chat here. The title will use the first user message automatically.

panel.title: Term entry details

experiences.help: One experience per line, in story order from top to bottom.
experiences.empty: No experiences yet. Add the first one below to track how this entry changes over the story.
experiences.addPlaceholder: Add an experience, press Enter to save

gallery.empty: No images yet — upload one, or open an image in chat / Codex and import it.

notes.label: Notes
notes.help: These notes are only visible to you and are not used in the generation of new prose.

external.title: External References
external.help: Add links to external sites like Google Docs, Notion or other places where you keep your information!

relations.title: Relations/Connections
relations.help: Add connections to other entries to build a network of information.

mentions.noManuscript: Mentions are available when your manuscript is loaded.

fields.aliasesHelp: Separate with commas. Names you use here will be detected in your prose.

planningStyleDescription.card: Use a highlighted card for planning.

reasoningStyleDescription.card: Use a highlighted card for reasoning.

infoPanel.title: Info Panel
infoPanel.previewEmptyHint: Click "Preview" to show the rendered prompt.

materials.empty: No materials yet. Click "Import" to add TXT files.

infoPanel.chatStartDescription: Choose a chat prompt and input values, then send your first message. The conversation title is generated from that first message.
infoPanel.chatPromptInvalid: Chat prompts must end with a user message containing exactly one chat.userInput placeholder.
infoPanel.chatPromptLocked: This chat has locked its prompt. Switching prompts mid-chat breaks cache reuse and reduces reply consistency. Start a new chat instead.
infoPanel.chatModelNoResponse: The model call completed, but returned no chat reply.
infoPanel.attachmentErrorType: Only JPG, PNG, and WebP images are supported
infoPanel.attachmentErrorVision: The current model does not support image input
infoPanel.attachmentVisionStripped: This conversation contains images, but the current model does not support image input — only text will be sent.
infoPanel.chatComposerPlaceholder: Type a message. Enter sends, Shift + Enter inserts a newline.

codex.emptyDescription: General sessions can be created freely. Scene operation and continuation sessions will be attached by their workflows later.
codex.boundCategoryEmpty: This category is created by its workflow.
codex.startTitle: Let Codex help write your novel
codex.composerPlaceholder: Let Codex help write your novel. @ to mention novel content, / to run a command

approval.acceptForSession: Yes, and don't ask again this session
approval.acceptWithPolicy: Yes, and don't ask again for commands that start with {policy}
approval.steer: No, and tell Codex what to do differently

chapter.label: Chapter
chapter.noChaptersYet: No chapters yet. Click "+ New Chapter" to add one.

codexSkillTweak.chatInputPlaceholder: Paste or type the content for the model to work on — @ mentioned terms are auto-injected…
codexSkillTweak.chatInputHint: This is assembled with the skill's bound prompt (overview + mentioned terms included automatically) and shipped with the message for Codex; the main composer text is your instruction to Codex.

sceneContinuation.codexInstructionPlaceholder: Extra guidance for Codex to act on…
sceneContinuation.sendHint: The inputs above are assembled into the prompt for Codex to act on; locked after sending
sceneContinuation.sentHint: Handed to Codex — keep adjusting in the session on the right

sceneOperation.emptySceneHint: The current scene is empty, so generation may not be useful.

deleteDialog.title: Confirm Delete
deleteDialog.description: Are you sure you want to delete chapter "{title}"? This action cannot be undone.

defaults.title: Default Prompts
defaults.hint: Each category can only use prompts with direct LLM calls enabled.

presets.title: Official Presets
presets.description: Built-in prompt presets are read-only. Clone one into your prompt library to edit it.

overwriteDialog.title: Overwrite existing prompts?
overwriteDialog.description: Some prompt names in this preset already exist. Continuing will overwrite those prompts in place.

publish.createDescription: Save the current prompt and its included components as a built-in JSON preset.
publish.overwriteDescription: Replace an existing preset with the current prompt and increment its version by 0.1.
publish.name: Preset name
publish.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this preset is for...

usages.intro: The following prompts include this component in their instructions:
usages.empty: No prompts are including this component yet.

editor.name: Name
editor.presetReadOnlyNotice: Read-only — clone it first to edit
editor.presetReadOnlyNoticeVersioned: Cloned from preset v{revision} — read-only, clone it first to edit
editor.multiMessageIntro: Each prompt needs to have at least one system message. Some prompt types also need additional user or assistant messages. In those cases, you can add as many additional messages as you need.
editor.componentIntro: Enter the instructions below that you want to reuse in other prompts. These instructions will be inserted into the prompt when you call it by its name.

templateReference.title: Template Reference
templateReference.intro: The prompt editor now uses standard Nunjucks syntax. These variables and tags are available directly in templates.
templateReference.novelLanguage: The language of the current novel, such as 中文 or English.
templateReference.novelOutline: Outputs the story-so-far novel summaries before the current execution point, including act and scene summaries.
templateReference.novelOutlineStorySoFar: Explicitly reads the story-so-far novel summaries before the current execution point; equivalent to `novel.outline`.
templateReference.novelOutlineFull: Reads all non-empty act summaries and scene summaries across the whole novel.
templateReference.instructionTermsText: Reads the names of terms linked to the current instruction.
templateReference.instructionTermsValue: Reads the detailed term blocks linked to the current instruction.
templateReference.chatUserInput: The current user input for this turn.
templateReference.chatUserInputTermsText: Reads the names of terms linked to the current user input, including always-included terms and excluding never-include terms.
templateReference.chatUserInputTermsValue: Reads the detailed term blocks linked to the current user input, including always-included terms and excluding never-include terms.
templateReference.chatHistory: The merged text of the current conversation history.
templateReference.chatHistoryTermsText: Reads the names of terms linked to the current conversation history, including always-included terms and excluding never-include terms.
templateReference.chatHistoryTermsValue: Reads the detailed term blocks linked to the current conversation history, including always-included terms and excluding never-include terms.
templateReference.scenePreviousText: The text that comes before the current scene.
templateReference.sceneFollowText: The text that comes after the current scene.
templateReference.sceneHasPreviousText: Whether the current scene has previous text, useful for conditionals.
templateReference.sceneChapterOutline: The detail outline of the chapter the current scene belongs to; only populated when that chapter outline is non-empty.
templateReference.sceneActOutline: The detail outline of the act the current scene belongs to; only populated when that act outline is non-empty.
templateReference.sceneHasChapterOutline: Whether the current scene's chapter has a non-empty outline, useful for conditionals.
templateReference.sceneHasActOutline: Whether the current scene's act has a non-empty outline, useful for conditionals.
templateReference.inputText: Reads the text value of an input.
templateReference.inputValue: Reads the value of an input (usually the raw option value or identifier).
templateReference.inputTermText: Reads the names of terms linked to an input (content selection only).
templateReference.inputTermValue: Reads the detailed term blocks linked to an input (content selection only).
templateReference.inputTermTagText: Reads the names of terms matched by term tags linked to an input (content selection only).
templateReference.inputTermTagValue: Reads the detailed term blocks for term tags linked to an input (content selection only).
templateReference.inputSnippetText: Reads the titles of snippets linked to an input (content selection only).
templateReference.inputSnippetValue: Reads the snippet bodies linked to an input (content selection only).
templateReference.inputFullNovelText: Reads the labels of selected full-novel entries linked to an input (content selection only).
templateReference.inputFullNovelValue: Reads the selected full-novel content linked to an input, following that input's full-text/summary mode (content selection only).
templateReference.inputActText: Reads the labels of selected acts linked to an input (content selection only).
templateReference.inputActValue: Reads the selected act content linked to an input, following that input's full-text/summary mode (content selection only).
templateReference.inputChapterText: Reads the labels of selected chapters linked to an input (content selection only).
templateReference.inputChapterValue: Reads the selected chapter content linked to an input, following that input's full-text/summary mode (content selection only).
templateReference.inputSceneText: Reads the labels of selected scenes linked to an input (content selection only).
templateReference.inputSceneValue: Reads the selected scene content linked to an input, following that input's full-text/summary mode (content selection only).
templateReference.inputActOutlineText: Reads the labels of selected act outlines linked to an input (content selection only).
templateReference.inputActOutlineValue: Reads the selected act outline bodies linked to an input (content selection only).
templateReference.inputChapterOutlineText: Reads the labels of selected chapter outlines linked to an input (content selection only).
templateReference.inputChapterOutlineValue: Reads the selected chapter outline bodies linked to an input (content selection only).
templateReference.unionTerms: Merges two term collections; the result also exposes `.count`, `.text`, and `.value`.
templateReference.chatUnionTerms: Example: merge terms detected from the current user input, chat history, and extra inputs, then read `.count`, `.text`, or `.value` from the combined set.
templateReference.includeComponent: Includes the content of a prompt component.

modelGroups.title: Model groups
modelGroups.hint: Attach model groups to extend this prompt with additional models.
modelGroups.setsHint: Attaching a model set automatically fills in its runnable model groups; embedding and reranker groups are ignored.

settings.title: Prompt settings
settings.llmCallHint: Allow this prompt to run as a single LLM call.
settings.agentCallHint: Allow this prompt to be referenced and called by a Codex skill (as its associated prompt).
settings.generateThenAgent: Hand off to Codex after generation

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.name: Name
inputs.unusedNotice: This input is not used by the prompt.
inputs.duplicateNameNotice: That name already exists. Kept the previous name.
inputs.includedNotice: This input is provided by an included prompt component and cannot be edited here.
inputs.includedNoticeWithSource: This input is provided by an included prompt component (“{name}”) and cannot be edited here.

allowedContent.title: Allowed content
allowedContent.help: Your input may contain more than one type of content.
allowedContent.customHint: Allows entering additional information not linked to a specific type of content.
allowedContent.contentSelectionHint: Select from a list of options based on your current novel/series.
allowedContent.checkboxHint: Shows a little checkbox next to the input.
allowedContent.textPlaceholderHint: Enter an example of the text that your prompt needs.
allowedContent.dropdownHint: Allow selecting from a predefined list.

contentSelection.title: Content options
contentSelection.hint: Choose which kinds of content users can select.
contentSelection.label: Label
contentSelection.displayNameHint: Optional. Leave blank to use the input name.

snippetPicker.title: Select snippets
snippetPicker.hint: Pick one or more snippets from this novel.

checkbox.defaultContentHint: Set the default checked state for this checkbox.

dropdown.title: Dropdown options
dropdown.colorHint: Optional. This is only used for UI display.
dropdown.advancedHint: Description is shown to users. Content is inserted into the prompt when this option is selected.
dropdown.optionDescriptionPlaceholder: Short help text for this option...

defaultContent.title: Default content
defaultContent.hint: This content will be used as the default value. You can use this to provide an example or a template.

preview.title: Preview
preview.renderedHint: Rendered with standard Nunjucks syntax, for example novel language, instruction text, scene text, and included components.
preview.missingRequiredBadge: Missing required: {names} · Unable to send to LLM
preview.warningUnclosedTemplateExpr: Unclosed template expression starting at position {pos}

export.title: Select clipboard format
export.description: This prompt includes other prompt components. How do you want to copy to the clipboard?

flatten.title: Copy without dependencies (inline components)
flatten.description: Replaces Nunjucks include references with component content so the prompt becomes self-contained.
flatten.unavailable: Cannot inline dependencies (missing/cycle/depth). Choose another format.

bundle.title: Copy full bundle (prompt + dependencies)
bundle.description: Copies the prompt together with all referenced components so it can be imported all at once.

asIs.title: Copy prompt as-is
asIs.description: Copies the prompt without changes. Any referenced components must exist when importing.

import.title: Import prompt bundle
import.description: Paste a prompt bundle or choose a JSON file to import it into your library.
import.conflictsTitle: Existing prompts that will be overwritten

overwriteDialog.title: Overwrite existing prompts?
overwriteDialog.description: These prompts already exist in your library. Continuing will overwrite them in place.

all.title: Import entire bundle
all.description: Imports the entry prompt and all included prompts in this bundle.

errors.flattenUnavailable: Cannot inline dependencies for this prompt
errors.nameAlreadyExists: Some prompt names already exist: {names}. Rename them and try again.
errors.duplicateNamesInBundle: This bundle contains duplicate prompt names: {names}.
errors.missingEntry: Entry prompt not found in bundle

errors.nameAlreadyExists: A prompt with this name already exists

editor.name: Name
editor.presetReadOnlyNotice: Read-only — clone it first to edit
editor.presetReadOnlyNoticeVersioned: Cloned from preset v{revision} — read-only, clone it first to edit
editor.associatedPromptHint: When Codex runs this skill it assembles this prompt into a draft and calls the model group (to use a model better suited for fiction).

editor.structureHint: `scripts/`, `references/`, and `assets/` are created automatically when a skill is added. They are read-only in the UI for now.
